title: Pilgrim's Road
provenance: From the artist’s studio, Saint John’s Abbey, Collegeville, Minnesota.
essay: Painted after Tupa’s walking pilgrimage along the Camino de Santiago, Pilgrim’s Road compresses weeks of landscape into a single fauvist panorama — ochre fields, a ribbon of red road, and the high blue of a Spanish sky. The horizon tilts as if seen mid-stride; the paint is applied in the broad, joyful strokes that have become the artist’s signature.

title: Silver Palace Tiles
provenance: From the artist’s studio, Saint John’s Abbey, Collegeville, Minnesota.
essay: A meditation on pattern and light, Silver Palace Tiles takes the geometry of Moorish tilework and lets it dissolve into pure color. Squares of teal and gold interlock, then break rank — an ordered surface interrupted by paint, the way ritual is interrupted by joy.

title: Abbey Bells at Dusk
provenance: From the artist’s studio, Saint John’s Abbey, Collegeville, Minnesota.
essay: The banner tower of Saint John’s Abbey — Marcel Breuer’s great concrete bell banner — rendered not as architecture but as sound: concentric waves of red and violet ringing outward across a darkening Minnesota sky. One of the largest canvases in the sale.

title: Umbrian Gate
provenance: Private collection, Minneapolis; acquired directly from the artist.
essay: From the artist’s Assisi period. A medieval gate in the Umbrian hills becomes a proscenium of hot yellow and rose, the passage beyond left ambiguous — an invitation rather than a destination. “We are all on a pilgrimage,” Tupa has said. “That is the human condition.”

title: The Long Walk to Compostela
provenance: From the artist’s studio, Saint John’s Abbey, Collegeville, Minnesota.
essay: A six-foot horizontal frieze — the widest work in the sale — tracing the pilgrim road west toward Santiago de Compostela. Waystations appear as flares of teal; the walking figures are barely more than brushmarks, dissolved into the landscape they cross.

title: Garden of the Cloister
provenance: From the artist’s studio, Saint John’s Abbey, Collegeville, Minnesota.
essay: An intimate study of the abbey garden in high summer — beds of red and gold held inside the cool geometry of the cloister walk. The smallest Tupa canvas in the sale, and among the most concentrated.

title: The Word Made Flesh — Illuminated Folio
medium: Ink, gold leaf and pigment on vellum (fine-art edition)
provenance: The Saint John’s Bible, Heritage Edition; Saint John’s University, Collegeville, Minnesota.
essay: A folio from The Saint John’s Bible — the first completely handwritten and illuminated Bible commissioned by a Benedictine abbey since the invention of the printing press. Gold leaf catches and returns the light of the room, as the illuminators intended.

title: Genesis Frontispiece — Illuminated Folio
medium: Ink, gold leaf and pigment on vellum (fine-art edition)
provenance: The Saint John’s Bible, Heritage Edition; Saint John’s University, Collegeville, Minnesota.
essay: The opening of Genesis: seven vertical bands of creation emerging from darkness into gold. Among the most reproduced images of the entire Saint John’s Bible project, offered here as a museum-grade illuminated folio.

title: Tea Bowl, Nanban-Glazed
essay: Fired in the Johanna Kiln — the largest wood-burning kiln in North America — at Saint John’s Pottery, where Bresnahan has been artist-in-residence since 1979. The nanban surface is unglazed by hand; the kiln itself paints it in ten days of continuous firing.

title: Sculpted Vase with Natural Ash Glaze
essay: Thrown from clay dug within thirty miles of the abbey and fired with deadfall timber from its forest — Bresnahan’s practice is a closed ecological loop, and every surface records the fire that finished it.

title: Original Work — Details Announced at Sale
provenance: Distinguished private collection. Full catalogue entry available at the evening sale.
essay: An original work by Pablo Picasso, offered from a distinguished private collection. In keeping with the consignor’s wishes, full details — title, date, medium and estimate — will be announced in the room on the evening of the sale.

title: Lakeshore, Minnesota
essay: A mid-century Minnesota lakeshore in late light, from a distinguished private collection — one of several regional works consigned in support of the abbey and university.

title: Still Life with Lilies
essay: Cut lilies against a field of loose, confident color — consigned from a private Twin Cities collection in support of the enduring missions of Saint John’s Abbey and Saint John’s University.
